title: How Uniface Prints
text: How Uniface Prints When printing a form or report, Uniface begins with the first occurrence it encounters in the component, reading vertically from top to bottom. When necessary, field and entity frames are expanded, so that data that does not fit in the frame is also sent to the print file. Printing takes place on a per line basis. As Uniface prints, it scans the component from left to right, top to bottom. When it encounters the top left corner of an occurrence of an entity, Uniface activates the getFocus trigger for that entity. If the current occurrence of that entity has not yet been fetched, Uniface activates the read trigger to fetch the data. As Uniface proceeds through the form, it formats each line into the print file. When printing with print modes All or Clear , field and entity frames are expanded, so that data that does not fit in the frame is also sent to the print file. If the entity's getFocus or leavePrinted trigger contains a printbreak statement, the print break frame's getFocus trigger is activated. For each occurrence that has been formatted to the print file, Uniface activates the leavePrinted trigger before proceeding to the next occurrence of the entity. If necessary, the read trigger is activated again to fetch the next occurrence of data. Related Links Design Guidelines for Printing Both forms and reports can be used for printing, but report components are the most appropriate choice when you need to provide specialized print control or batch processing. A report component does not allow user interaction or database I/O. Layouts for Printing The following examples illustrate the effect that the component layout has on the printed output. Triggers for Printing Much report writing is data-driven—you might want to print a number of different things in different situations, depending on the data in the report. This involves using conditional ProcScript that checks what is happening in the form and directs the print process accordingly. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
